changes_made: Removed k8s_inventory_pod_lifecycle_logging_enabled as it does not follow naming conventions and overlaps with audit functions.
review_notes: The control CM-8 (1) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves maintaining an updated inventory of system components, which can be achieved through audit logging of pod and node lifecycle events. The function 'k8s_inventory_pod_lifecycle_logging_enabled' was removed due to naming inconsistency and redundancy. The remaining functions are well-named and accurately reflect the audit logging requirements for maintaining an inventory of system components.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names follow the k8s_<resource>_<check_description> pattern for clarity and consistency.
title: System Component Inventory | Updates During Installation and Removal
step1_reasoning: The control CM-8 (1) focuses on maintaining an updated inventory of system components during installation and removal. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that pod and node lifecycle events (creation, deletion) are logged and monitored. This involves enabling audit logging for these events to maintain an accurate inventory of active components in the cluster.
